<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Limited">
[Glowing Trail](/Spells.aspx?ID=1428)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

Your movements leave a vague glowing path behind you, like a misty echo of your presence. While your form is not discernible, your size is. This path hangs in space and is not anchored to any surface, so it tracks your movement through the air. The path fades after 10 minutes. You can Dismiss this spell at any time, but the path fades normally.

 The path can be visible or invisible. While visible, it appears as a faintly glowing mist in a color of your choosing but sheds no light beyond its area. While invisible, you can still detect the path, but it gives off no light.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The _glowing trail_ fades after 1 day.

**Heightened (5th)** The _glowing trail_ fades after 1 week.

**Heightened (7th)** The _glowing trail_ fades after 1 month.

**Heightened (9th)** The _glowing trail_ fades after 1 year.
name: Glowing Trail

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="">
[Know Location](/Spells.aspx?ID=1429)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #194: Cult of the Cave Worm](/Sources.aspx?ID=209) pg. 79

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute ([somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283),  [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284))

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 1 mile

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the next time you make your daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You create an invisible anchor at a location within range (even if it's outside your line of sight or line of effect), as long as you can identify the location by its appearance (or other identifying features). You innately know the direction towards that location, including relative depth, but not the distance.

 Incorrect knowledge of the location's appearance usually causes the spell to fail, but it could instead lead to an unwanted location or some other unusual mishap determined by the GM. This spell doesn't help you find a suitable route to the location nor assist you in overcoming obstacles on the way there.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The range is 10 miles.

**Heightened (5th)** The range is 100 miles.

**Heightened (7th)** The range is planetary and you can create an anchor at a location you've viewed with [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268) or similar effects.
name: Know Location

May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb **Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 81 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 100 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Fortitude --- A shimmering muzzle of pure magical force clamps the target's mouth shut. The target can attempt a Fortitude save to resist the muzzle. For targets with more than one mouth, the caster must target a single mouth. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** For 1 round, the target can't use jaws Strikes, Breath Weapons, or any other attack that originates from the mouth, nor can they imbibe anything, including [potions](/Traits.aspx?ID=130), [mutagens](/Traits.aspx?ID=116), and [elixirs](/Traits.aspx?ID=59).
**Failure** As success, but the effect lasts 1 minute, and they must succeed at a DC 11 flat check to Cast a Spell with verbal components.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the effect lasts 1 minute and the target can't speak. --- **Heightened (5th)** You can target up to 5 creatures.
name: Binding Muzzle

<title level="1" right="Spell 9" pfs="">
[Call Fluxwraith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1434)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Wake the Dead #3](/Sources.aspx?ID=212) pg. 25

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

You break a mirror and cast the shards into the air. Between their thousand fractured reflections, the fluxwraith appears—a thing of broken glass, broken time and broken lives. This undead creature occupies the space of a Medium creature that has a fly Speed of 60 feet.

**Arrive** ([transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) _Interrupted Lives_ Each enemy within a 10-foot emanation must attempt a Will save. If a creature fails its save, it is shunted into the future, vanishing now but automatically reappearing in the same location after 1d4 rounds. A creature can attempt an additional Will save at the end of each of its turns to end the duration. If its original space is occupied when it returns, the creature appears in the nearest unoccupied space. Since the creature is shunted forward in time, it doesn't experience any of the intervening time. Likewise, any effects on the creature with a duration don't decrease their duration, resuming when the creature reappears.

**Depart** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=132)) _Dire Foresight_ Each enemy within a 60-foot emanation must attempt a Will save. On a failed save, the creature recieves a brief vision of a deeply traumatic event that lurks among the many possibilities of its future. The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 2 (frightened 4 on a critical failure) and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 while they are frightened.
name: Call Fluxwraith

markdown:
**Source** [Wake the Dead #3](/Sources.aspx?ID=212) pg. 25 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Will --- You hold up a fractured mirror to the target, and in that mirror it sees a thing that may yet come to pass—a ghastly fortelling that the target instinctively knows is eerily possible. It's not a guaranteed thing, just a dreadful possibility, but it haunts the targets's thoughts all the same. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is slowed 1 and stupefied 1 until it next gets eight hours of rest.
**Critical Failure** The target is slowed 2 and stupefied 2, decreasing these conditions by 1 for each eight hours of rest it gets.
name: Heinous Future

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 314 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Telvrys](/Deities.aspx?ID=668), [The Devourer](/Deities.aspx?ID=339) **Range** 120 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Reflex --- An ephemeral, taloned hand grips the target, burning it with magical acid. The target takes 2d8 acid damage plus 1d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) depending on its Reflex save. A creature taking persistent damage from this spell takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and no persistent damage, and the claw [moves](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) it up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and full persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 20 feet in a direction of your choice. --- **Heightened (+2)** The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.
name: Acid Grip

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 314 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Andoletta](/Deities.aspx?ID=464), [Horus](/Deities.aspx?ID=516), [Mother Vulture](/Deities.aspx?ID=542), [Nephthys](/Deities.aspx?ID=637), [Pazuzu](/Deities.aspx?ID=434), [Ranginori](/Deities.aspx?ID=693), [Tanagaar](/Deities.aspx?ID=654), [Tjasse](/Deities.aspx?ID=498), [Uvuko](/Deities.aspx?ID=682), [Ylimancha](/Deities.aspx?ID=488) **Duration** 1 minute --- You harness your mastery of the sky to reshape your body into a Medium flying animal battle form. When you Cast this Spell, choose a listed battle form. You can decide the specific type of animal (such as an owl or eagle for bird), but this has no effect on the form's Size or statistics. While in this form, you gain the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait. You can Dismiss the spell.
You gain specific abilities based on the animal you choose:
  • AC = 18 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 5 temporary Hit Points.
  • [Low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416).
  • One or more [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee attacks specific to the battle form you choose, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +16, and your damage bonus is +5. These attacks are Dexterity based (for the purpose of the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) condition, for example). If your attack modifier for Dexterity-based unarmed attacks is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) modifier of +16, unless your own is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the form you choose: - **Bat** Speed 20 feet, fly 30 feet; precise echolocation 40 feet; **Melee** fangs, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; **Melee** wing ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6 bludgeoning. - **Bird** Speed 10 feet, fly 50 feet; **Melee** beak, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; **Melee** talon ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d10 slashing. - **Pterosaur** Speed 10 feet, fly 40 feet; imprecise scent 30 feet; Melee beak, Damage 3d6 piercing. - **Wasp** Speed 20 feet, fly 40 feet; **Melee** stinger, **Damage** 1d8 piercing plus 1d6 persistent poison. --- **Heightened (5th)** Your battle form is Large and your fly Speed gains a +10-foot status bonus. You instead gain 10 temporary HP, attack modifier +18, damage bonus +8, and Acrobatics +20. **Heightened (6th)** Your battle form is Huge, your fly Speed gains a +15-foot status bonus, and your attacks have 10-foot reach. You instead gain AC = 21 + your level, 15 temporary HP, attack modifier +21, damage bonus +4 and double damage dice (including persistent damage), and Acrobatics +23.
name: Aerial Form

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 314

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 8 hours
</row>

</column>

---

You ward an area to alert you when creatures enter without your permission. When you cast alarm, select a password. Whenever a Small or larger corporeal creature enters the spell's area without speaking the password, alarm sends your choice of a mental alert (in which case the spell gains the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) trait) or an audible alarm with the sound and volume of a hand bell (in which case the spell gains the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait). Either option automatically awakens you, and the bell allows each creature in the area to attempt a DC 15 Perception check to wake up. A creature aware of the alarm must succeed at a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against the spell's DC or trigger the spell when moving into the area.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** You can specify a trigger for which types of creatures sound the alarm spell, as described in Setting Triggers on page 303.
name: Alarm

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 315 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Ananshea](/Deities.aspx?ID=592), [Angazhan](/Deities.aspx?ID=644), [Cernunnos](/Deities.aspx?ID=513), [Drokalion](/Deities.aspx?ID=702), [Geryon](/Deities.aspx?ID=398), [Kagia](/Deities.aspx?ID=607), [Kazutal](/Deities.aspx?ID=350), [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=287), [Ravithra](/Deities.aspx?ID=620), [Rowdrosh](/Deities.aspx?ID=652), [Stag Mother of the Forest of Stones](/Deities.aspx?ID=609), [Wadjet](/Deities.aspx?ID=643), [Yrmidar](/Deities.aspx?ID=500) **Duration** 1 minute --- You call upon primal energy to transform yourself into a Medium animal battle form. When you Cast this Spell, choose a listed battle form. You can decide the specific type of animal (such as lion or snow leopard for cat), but this has no effect on the form's Size or statistics. While in this form, you gain the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait. You can Dismiss the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which animal you choose:
  • AC = 16 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 5 temporary Hit Points.
  • [Low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416) and [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) 30 feet.
  • One or more [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) melee attacks specific to your battle form, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +9, and your damage bonus is +1. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your unarmed attack bonus is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +9, unless your own is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the animal you choose: Player Core - **Ape** Speed 25 feet, climb 20 feet; Melee fist, Damage 2d6 bludgeoning. - **Bear** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; **Melee** claw (agile), **Damage** 1d8 slashing. - **Bull** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** horn, **Damage** 2d8 piercing. - **Canine** Speed 40 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d8 piercing. - **Cat** Speed 40 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d6 piercing; **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d10 slashing. - **Deer** Speed 50 feet; **Melee** antler, **Damage** 2d6 piercing. - **Frog** Speed 25 feet, swim 25 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d6 bludgeoning; **Melee** tongue ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d4 bludgeoning. - **Shark** swim 35 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; breathe underwater but not in air. - **Snake** Speed 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 20 feet; **Melee** fangs, **Damage** 2d4 piercing plus 1d6 poison. Howl of the Wild - **Crab** Speed 25 feet, swim Speed 15 ft; **Melee** big claw, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; Melee little claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d4 piercing; can breathe in water and in air. - **Crocodile** Speed 25 feet, swim Speed 30 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; **Melee** tail ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d8 bludgeoning; can hold your breath for the duration of the transformation. - **Orca** swim Speed 35 feet; **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d8 piercing; can hold your breath for the duration of the transformation. - **Seal** Speed 20 feet, swim Speed 30 feet; **Melee** jaws ([grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619)), **Damage** 2d6 piercing; can hold your breath for the duration of the transformation. --- **Heightened (3rd)** You instead gain 10 temporary HP, AC = 17 + your level, attack modifier +14, damage bonus +5, and Athletics +14. **Heightened (4th)** Your battle form is Large and your attacks have 10-foot reach. You instead gain 15 temporary HP, AC = 18 + your level, attack modifier +16, damage bonus +9, and Athletics +16. **Heightened (5th)** Your battle form is Huge and your attacks have 15-foot reach. You instead gain 20 temporary HP, AC = 18 + your level, attack modifier +18, damage bonus +7 and double the number of damage dice, and Athletics +20.
name: Animal Form

[Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 315

[Zeaki](/Deities.aspx?ID=707)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** see text
</row>

</column>

---

You offer food, and an ordinary Tiny animal within range approaches to eat it. You imprint the image, direction, and distance of an obvious place or landmark well known to you within the animal. You can also attach a small object or note up to light Bulk to it. The animal does its best to reach the destination; if it makes it there, it waits nearby and allows nonhostile creatures to approach and remove the attached object. The spell ends after the message is delivered or after 24 hours, whichever comes first. If there are no Tiny wild animals in range, the spell is lost.
name: Animal Messenger

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 315 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Aegirran](/Deities.aspx?ID=490), [Gogunta](/Deities.aspx?ID=321), [Hanspur](/Deities.aspx?ID=347), [Hataam](/Deities.aspx?ID=596), [Lysianassa](/Deities.aspx?ID=692), [Obari](/Deities.aspx?ID=356), [Ristrentho](/Deities.aspx?ID=608), [Waves of the Boundless Sea](/Deities.aspx?ID=375) **Range** 60 feet **Defense** Reflex **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- A sphere of water 10 feet in diameter forms in an unoccupied space in range, either on the ground or on the surface of a liquid. When you Cast this Spell and each time you Sustain it, you can roll the orb, moving it up to 10 feet along the ground or the surface of a liquid. The orb can move through the spaces of any creatures or obstacles that wouldn't stop the flow of water. It extinguishes non-magical fires it moves through of its size or smaller, and it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any magical fires it moves through. If it fails to counteract a given fire, it can't counteract that fire for the duration of the spell. The orb can engulf Large or smaller creatures it moves through, and it can contain as many creatures as fit in its space. The orb can try to engulf the same creature only once per turn, even if you roll it onto a creature's space more than once. Any Large or smaller creature whose space the orb tries to move through can attempt a Reflex save. **Success** The creature can either let the orb pass (remaining in its space or moving out of the orb's path into a space of the creature's choice) or allow itself to be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) in front of the orb to the end of the orb's movement.
**Failure** The creature is engulfed in the orb. It moves along with the orb and must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (unless it can breathe in water). An engulfed Medium or smaller creature and anyone trying to affect that creature follow the normal rules for [aquatic battles](/Rules.aspx?ID=467). An engulfed Large creature is usually big enough that parts of it stick out from the water, and it can reach out of the water.
The creature can get free either by [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) with a successful DC 10 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check or by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) against your spell DC. A freed creature exits the orb's space and can immediately breathe.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature can't Swim to get free.
name: Aqueous Orb

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 316 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Area** 120-foot line **Defense** Fortitude --- A jagged crack opens in the air, dealing 12d8 cold damage as it draws away warmth. Each creature along the rift must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) until the start of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) by a layer of ice, and is slowed 1 as long as its immobilized. The ice is an object with 60 Hit Points, Hardness 5, immunity to cold damage, and vulnerability 10 to fire. It has object immunities (page 269) and is destroyed if the target [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d8 and the ice's Hit Points increase by 5.
name: Arctic Rift

<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=132" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 316

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a vague glimpse of the future. During the casting of this spell, ask about the results of a particular course of action. The spell can predict results up to 30 minutes into the future and reveal the GM's best guess among the following outcomes: good, bad, mixed (the results will be a mix of good and bad), and nothing (there won't be particularly good or bad results).

 The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) DC 6 flat check. On a failure, the result is always “nothing.” This makes it impossible to tell whether a “nothing” result is accurate. If anyone asks about the same topic as the first casting of augury during an additional casting, the GM uses the secret roll result from the first casting. If circumstances change, though, it's possible to get a different result.
name: Augury

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 316 **Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Duration** 1 minute --- You transform into an avatar of your deity, assuming a Huge battle form. You have hands in this battle form and can take manipulate actions. You can Dismiss this spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which deity's battle form you assume:
  • AC = 25 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 30 temporary Hit Points.
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more attacks specific to your deity's battle form, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +33, and you use the listed damage. Melee attacks are Strength based (for the purposes of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example) unless they have the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179) trait, and all ranged attacks are Dexterity based.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +35, unless your own is higher.
You also gain the specific abilities listed for your deity below: Player Core - **[Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277)** Speed 50 feet, burrow 30 feet, immune to [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24); **Ranged** crossbow ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 6d10+3 piercing. - **[Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278)** Speed 70 feet, fly 70 feet; Melee mace ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 6d10+6 bludgeoning; **Ranged** hellfire ([range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6+3 fire. - **[Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=279)** Speed 30 feet, fly 70 feet; **Melee** whip ([disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 6d4+6 slashing; **Ranged** savored sting ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6+3 poison. - **[Cayden Cailean](/Deities.aspx?ID=280)** Speed 70 feet, fly 70 feet, ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain; **Melee** rapier ([deadly 3d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 6d6+6 piercing; **Ranged** ale splash ([range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6+3 poison. - **[Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281)** Speed 30 feet, fly 70 feet; **Melee** starknife ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly 3d4](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=369), [thrown 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 6d4+6 piercing; **Ranged** moonbeam ([range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)), **Damage** 6d6+3 fire. - **[Erastil](/Deities.aspx?ID=282)** Speed 70 feet, fly 70 feet, ignore [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain; <title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 317

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 10-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
Will

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You fill the minds of your enemies with doubt. Enemies in the area must succeed at a Will save or take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls as long as they are in the area. Once per round on subsequent turns, you can Sustain the spell to increase the emanation's radius by 10 feet and force enemies in the area that weren't yet affected to attempt another saving throw. _Bane_ can counteract [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451).
name: Bane

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 317 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature that isn't on its home plane **Defense** Will --- You send the target back to its home plane. The target must attempt a Will save. You can spend an extra action while Casting this Spell and add a cost to the spell to give the creature a –2 circumstance penalty to its save. The cost must be a specially gathered object that is anathema to the creature. This spell fails if you aren't on your home plane when you cast it. **Critical Success** The target resists being banished and you are [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Success** The target resists being banished.
**Failure** The target is banished.
**Critical Failure** The target is banished and can't return by any means to the plane it's banished from for 1 week. --- **Heightened (9th)** You can target up to 10 creatures. The extra cost affects targets to which it is anathema.
name: Banishment

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />

<column gap="tiny">

[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

[Undead](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=28)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 mindless undead creature with a level no greater than  bind undead ’s spell rank
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 day
</row>

</column>

---

With a word of power, you seize control of the target. It gains the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=109) trait. If you or an ally uses any [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the target, the spell ends.
name: Bind Undead

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Blazing Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450)
<actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 318

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 or more creatures
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC

</row>

</column>

---

You fire a ray of heat and flame. Make a spell attack roll against a single creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage, and on a critical hit, the target takes double damage.

 For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, you can fire an additional ray at a different target, to a maximum of three rays targeting three different targets for 3 actions.

 These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls for blazing bolt. If you spend 2 or more actions Casting the Spell, the damage increases to 4d6 fire damage on a hit, and it still deals double damage on a critical hit.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage to each target increases by 1d6 for the 1-action version, or by 2d6 for the 2- and 3-action versions.
name: Blazing Bolt

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 318 **Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Bloodline** [Angelic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=20) **Range** 120 feet **Area** up to a 60-foot burst **Defense** Reflex **Duration** 1 minute --- Manifestations of divine force appear in the hundreds, swirling in a massive, protective sphere. These typically look like spiky fragments, but often take on an appearance themed to the deity of the caster. The sphere is hollow, with the manifestations forming a shell 2 inches deep on the outer edge. You can choose to make the burst smaller, in 5-foot increments, when you cast it. The shell provides [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) and can intersect solid terrain without affecting it. The shell deals 7d8 force damage to each creature who intersects with the shell when the sphere's created, or who attempts to move through the shell. The creature also takes the damage at the end of its turn, but only if it didn't already take damage from the shell that turn. The effects are determined by a creature's Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) up to 10 feet in the direction of your choice, and ends its movement.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is pushed up to 20 feet in the direction of your choice, and ends its movement. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d8.
name: Blessed Boundary

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 318 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Trigger** A creature deals piercing, slashing, or persistent bleed damage to you **Requirements** You can bleed **Range** 30 feet **Target** the triggering creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You curse the target, punishing it for having the audacity to spill your blood. The target takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half the persistent bleed damage.
**Failure** The target takes the full persistent bleed damage. Until the bleeding stops, the target has weakness 1 to piercing and slashing damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target takes double the persistent bleed damage. --- **Heightened (+2)** The persistent bleed damage increases by 2d6.
name: Blood Vendetta

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** the triggering creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Your blessing revives a creature at the moment of its death. You prevent the target from dying and restore 5d8 Hit Points to the target. You can't use breath of life if the triggering effect was a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect or an effect that leaves no remains, such as [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76).

---
**Heightened (+2)** The healing increases by 1d8.
name: Breath of Life

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 319 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Range** 120 feet **Area** 10-foot burst **Defense** Will **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You forcibly calm creatures in the area, soothing them into a nonviolent state; each creature must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** Calming urges impose a –1 status penalty to the creature's attack rolls.
**Failure** Any [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects that would affect the creature are suppressed and the creature can't use [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300). If the target is subject to hostility from any other creature, it ceases to be affected by _calm_.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but hostility doesn't end the effect.
name: Calm

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 319 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Bloodlines** [Demonic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=21), [Undead](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=28) **Deity** [Otilaz](/Deities.aspx?ID=718) **Range** 120 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** varies --- You create a melody distilled from pure grief, conveying the inevitable loss of everything your target cherishes, audible to only them. The target takes 10d6 mental damage depending on its Will save. A creature cursed by this spell can't benefit from circumstance or status bonuses, for the duration noted in the degree of success. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage, is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), and is cursed for 1 round.
**Failure** The target takes full damage, is frightened 3, and is cursed for 1 week.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, is frightened 4, and is cursed for an unlimited duration. While the curse remains, the target's allies are also affected by the curse while within 15 feet of the creature.
name: Canticle of Everlasting Grief

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 319 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Range** 1,000 feet **Area** 60-foot burst **Defense** [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex --- You call upon the unimaginable power of world-ending cataclysms, ripping a small piece of each cataclysm and combining them together into one horrifically powerful attack. The following effects come down upon all creatures in the area. Treat the resistances of creatures in the area as if they were 10 lower for the purpose of determining the cataclysm's damage. Each creature attempts one basic Reflex save that applies to all five types of damage.
  • Flesh-dissolving acid rain deals 3d10 acid damage.
  • A roaring earthquake shakes and bludgeons creatures on the ground, dealing 3d10 bludgeoning damage.
  • A blast of freezing wind deals 3d10 cold damage.
  • Incredible lightning lashes the area, dealing 3d10 electricity damage.
  • Beating winds churn across the sky, dealing 3d10 bludgeoning damage to creatures flying in the area.
  • An instant tsunami sweeps over creatures in the area, dealing 3d10 bludgeoning damage (doubled for creatures swimming in the area).
  • A massive wildfire burns in a sudden inferno, dealing 3d10 fire damage.
name: Cataclysm

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 319

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deities**
[Anras](/Deities.aspx?ID=708), [Cixyron](/Deities.aspx?ID=417), [Dagon](/Deities.aspx?ID=429), [Hei Feng](/Deities.aspx?ID=307), [Ranginori](/Deities.aspx?ID=693), [Rull](/Deities.aspx?ID=559), [Vineshvakhi](/Deities.aspx?ID=622), [Weight of the World](/Deities.aspx?ID=384)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

**Target** 1 creature, plus any number of additional creatures
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You discharge a powerful bolt of lightning at the target, dealing 8d12 electricity damage. The target must attempt a basic Reflex save. The electricity then arcs to another creature within  30 feet of the first target, jumps to another creature within 30 feet of that target, and so on. You can end the chain at any point. You can't target the same creature more than once, and you must have line of effect to all targets. Roll the damage only once and apply it to each target (halving or doubling as appropriate for its saving throw outcome). The chain ends if any one of the targets critically succeeds at its save.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d12.
name: Chain Lightning

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 320 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Bloodlines** [Diabolic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=22), [Fey](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=25) **Deities** [Ardad Lili](/Deities.aspx?ID=588), [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), [Belial](/Deities.aspx?ID=396), [Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=279), [Conqueror Worm](/Deities.aspx?ID=234), [Corosbel](/Deities.aspx?ID=418), [Hathor](/Deities.aspx?ID=634), [Kugaptee](/Deities.aspx?ID=725), [Nalinivati](/Deities.aspx?ID=313), [Nyarlathotep (The Faceless Sphinx)](/Deities.aspx?ID=574), [Ozranvial](/Deities.aspx?ID=599), [Ravithra](/Deities.aspx?ID=620), [Razmir](/Deities.aspx?ID=357), [Sturovenen](/Deities.aspx?ID=523), [The Green Mother](/Deities.aspx?ID=453), [Ussharassim](/Deities.aspx?ID=531), [Velgaas](/Deities.aspx?ID=601), [Zura](/Deities.aspx?ID=443) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** 1 hour --- To the target, your words are honey and your visage seems bathed in a dreamy haze. It must attempt a Will save, with a +4 circumstance bonus if you or your allies recently threatened it or used [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against it. You can Dismiss the spell. If you use hostile actions against the target, the spell ends. When the spell ends, the target doesn't necessarily realize it was charmed unless its friendship with you or the actions you convinced it to take clash with its expectations, meaning you could potentially convince the target to continue being your friend via mundane means. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected and aware you tried to charm it.
**Success** The target is unaffected but thinks your spell was something harmless instead of _charm_, unless it [identifies the spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2249).
**Failure** The target's attitude becomes [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18) toward you. If it was friendly, it becomes [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21). It can't use hostile actions against you.
**Critical Failure** The target's attitude becomes helpful toward you, and it can't use hostile actions against you. --- **Heightened (4th)** The duration lasts until your next daily preparations. **Heightened (8th)** The duration lasts until your next daily preparations, and you can target up to 10 creatures.
name: Charm

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 320 **Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Lesson** [Lesson of Shadow](/Lessons.aspx?ID=22) **Range** 120 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** AC --- You shoot an utterly cold ray of darkness tinged with unholy energy. Make a ranged spell attack. The ray deals 5d6 cold damage. If the target has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait, you deal an extra 5d6 spirit damage. **Critical Success** The target takes double damage.
**Success** The target takes full damage.

If the ray passes through an area of magical light or targets a creature affected by magical [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), _chilling darkness_ attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the light. If you need to determine whether the ray passes through an area of light, draw a line between yourself and the spell's target. --- **Heightened (+1)** The cold damage increases by 2d6, and the spirit damage against holy creatures increases by 2d6.
name: Chilling Darkness

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 320

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Gentle restorative magic pushes back the effects of toxins and more complex maladies. Choose an affliction on the target, such as a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126). If it has advanced past stage one, reduce the stage by one. This reduction can be applied only once to a given case of an affliction, with the case ending when it's completely cured. Although the reduction can't occur again, heightened versions of this spell attempt to counteract with each casting.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** Attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the affliction if it is a disease or poison.

**Heightened (4th)** Attempt to counteract the affliction if it is a curse, disease, or poison.
name: Cleanse Affliction

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Cleanse Cuisine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 320

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 10 feet

**Area** 1 cubic foot

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You transform all food and beverages in the area into delicious fare, changing water into wine or another fine beverage, or enhancing the food's taste and ingredients to make it a gourmet treat. You can also choose to remove all toxins and contaminations from the food. This spell doesn't prevent future contamination, natural decay, or spoilage, nor does it make the food any more nutritious.

---
**Heightened (+2)** Add another cubic foot to the area, which must be contiguous with the rest.
name: Cleanse Cuisine

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 320

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You drive mental contamination from the target's mind. Attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) an effect of your choice imposing one of these conditions on the target: [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). If you failed to counteract the effect but you would have if its counteract rank were 2 lower, instead suppress the effect until the beginning of your next turn. The effect's duration doesn't elapse while it's suppressed. This spell can't counteract or suppress conditions that are part of curses, diseases, or a natural state of the target.

---
**Heightened (4th)** Add [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) to the list of conditions.

**Heightened (6th)** As 4th rank, plus add [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67).

**Heightened (8th)** As 4th rank, plus add [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
name: Clear Mind

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 321 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Patron Themes** [Faith's Flamekeeper](/Patrons.aspx?ID=12), [The Unseen Broker](/Patrons.aspx?ID=26), [The Unseen Broker](/Patrons.aspx?ID=29) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** until the end of the target's next turn --- You shout a command that's hard to ignore. You can command the target to approach you, run away (as if it had the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition), release what it's holding, drop [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), or stand in place. It can't [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=77) or take any reactions until it has obeyed your command. The effects depend on the target's Will save. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature must spend the first action on its next turn to obey your command.
**Critical Failure** The target must use all its actions on its next turn to obey your command. --- **Heightened (5th)** You can target up to 10 creatures.
name: Command

The effects are determined by the target's Will save. You can Dismiss the spell. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target babbles incoherently and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confusion.
**Critical Failure** The target is confused for 1 minute, with no save to end early. --- **Heightened (8th)** You can target up to 10 creatures.
name: Confusion

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Contingency](/Spells.aspx?ID=1472)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 321

**Tradition**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

**Deities**
[Abraxas](/Deities.aspx?ID=425), [Aerekostes](/Deities.aspx?ID=699), [Aroden](/Deities.aspx?ID=512), [Dalenydra](/Deities.aspx?ID=469), [Nalinivati](/Deities.aspx?ID=313), [Nyarlathotep (The Veiled Voice)](/Deities.aspx?ID=575), [Valmallos](/Deities.aspx?ID=147)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You prepare a spell that will trigger later. While casting contingency, you also cast another spell of 4th rank or lower with a casting time of no more than 3 actions. This companion spell must be one that can affect you. You must make any decisions for the spell when you cast contingency, such as choosing a damage type for [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256). During the casting, choose a trigger under which the spell will be cast, using the same restrictions as for the trigger of a [Ready](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) action. Once _contingency_ is cast, you can cause the companion spell to come into effect as a reaction with that trigger. It affects only you, even if it would affect more creatures. If you define complicated conditions, as determined by the GM, the trigger might fail. If you cast _contingency_ again, the newer casting supersedes the older.

---
**Heightened (8th)** You can choose a spell of 5th rank or lower.

**Heightened (10th)** You can choose a spell of 7th rank or lower.

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Cozy Cabin](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Wood" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=508" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 321

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Aminara](/Deities.aspx?ID=664), [Elion](/Deities.aspx?ID=405), [Emmeton Galardaria](/Deities.aspx?ID=341), [Good Neighbors](/Deities.aspx?ID=368), [Immonhiel](/Deities.aspx?ID=626), [Malthus](/Deities.aspx?ID=528), [The Offering Plate](/Deities.aspx?ID=254), [The Spirit Wall](/Deities.aspx?ID=363), [Uskyeria](/Deities.aspx?ID=485), [Yelayne](/Deities.aspx?ID=630)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 12 hours
</row>

</column>

---

You shape a cabin 20 feet on each side and 10 feet high. This cabin has the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait and the same restrictions as magic items that create structures. The walls of the hut are simple and wooden, with small, square glass windows, and it has one wooden door. It doesn't include its own lock, but it has a fastener to which a lock can be applied.

 The interior contains three cots, one chamber pot, and a small fireplace holding a magical fire. The interior is lit with a small magical light that you can light or extinguish at will using a Sustain action. The climate inside the hut is comfortable and allows creatures inside it to withstand most hostile weather conditions, but incredible heat or cold, powerful storms, and winds of hurricane force or greater destroy the hut. Other creatures can freely enter and exit the hut without damaging it, but if you exit the hut, the spell ends. You can Dismiss the spell.
name: Cozy Cabin

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

---

You create enough food to feed six Medium creatures for a day. This food is bland and unappealing, but it is nourishing. After 1 day, if no one has eaten the food, it decays and becomes inedible. Most Small creatures eat one-quarter as much as a Medium creature (one-sixteenth as much for most Tiny creatures), and most Large creatures eat 10 times as much (100 times as much for Huge creatures and so on).

---
**Heightened (4th)** You can feed 12 Medium creatures.

**Heightened (6th)** You can feed 50 Medium creatures.

**Heightened (8th)** You can feed 200 Medium creatures.
name: Create Food

<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 322

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deities**
[Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277), [Aesocar](/Deities.aspx?ID=403), [Alglenweis](/Deities.aspx?ID=605), [Apsu](/Deities.aspx?ID=330), [Bharnarol](/Deities.aspx?ID=467), [Bolka](/Deities.aspx?ID=445), [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=333), [Demon Bringers](/Deities.aspx?ID=230), [Inna](/Deities.aspx?ID=539), [Isis](/Deities.aspx?ID=517), [Liisglan](/Deities.aspx?ID=540), [Mammon](/Deities.aspx?ID=399), [Nocticula](/Deities.aspx?ID=724), [Radiant Prism](/Deities.aspx?ID=380), [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293), [Stone's Blood](/Deities.aspx?ID=382), [Sun Wukong](/Deities.aspx?ID=316), [The Endless Road](/Deities.aspx?ID=246), [The Laborer's Bastion](/Deities.aspx?ID=253), [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=294), [Varg](/Deities.aspx?ID=561), [Wheels of Innovation](/Deities.aspx?ID=385), [Wulgren](/Deities.aspx?ID=562), [Zogmugot](/Deities.aspx?ID=508)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 0 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure a temporary object from magical energy. It must consist of earthen or plant-derived matter (such as wood, paper, brick, or stone) and be 5 cubic feet or smaller. It can't rely on intricate artistry or complex moving parts, never fulfills a cost or the like, and can't be made of [precious](/Traits.aspx?ID=131) materials or materials with a rarity of [uncommon](/Traits.aspx?ID=159) or higher. It is obviously temporarily conjured, and thus can't be sold or passed off as a genuine item. The spell gains the appropriate trait for the item created, typically [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508).

---
**Heightened (5th)** The item is metal and can include common minerals, like feldspar or quartz. The spell gains the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait if used to create a metal object.
name: Creation

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 322 **Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will --- You assault the target's faith, riddling the creature with doubt and mental turmoil that deal 6d6 mental damage, or 6d8 mental damage if it can cast [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells. The effects are determined by its Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage; if the target can cast divine spells, it's [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, is stupefied 1 for 1 round, and can't cast divine spells for 1 round. To many deities, casting this spell on a follower of your own deity without significant cause is anathema. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6 (or by 2d8 if the target is a divine spellcaster).
name: Crisis of Faith

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 322 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Bloodline** [Hag](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=26) **Deities** [Mestama](/Deities.aspx?ID=519), [Mother Vulture](/Deities.aspx?ID=542), [The Lantern King](/Deities.aspx?ID=455), [Venkelvore](/Deities.aspx?ID=506), [Yig](/Deities.aspx?ID=579), [Zeaki](/Deities.aspx?ID=707) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- You transform the target creature into a harmless animal appropriate to the area, with effects based on its Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's body gains minor features of the harmless animal. Its insides churn as they partially transform, causing it to be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). When it recovers from the sickened condition, its features revert to normal.
**Failure** The target transforms for 1 minute but keeps its mind. If it spends all its actions on its turn concentrating on its original form, it can attempt a Will save to end the effect immediately.
**Critical Failure** The target is transformed into the chosen harmless animal, body and mind, for an unlimited duration.
name: Cursed Metamorphosis

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Darkness" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=39" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 322

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a shroud of darkness that prevents light from penetrating or emanating within the area. Light does not enter the area and any non-magical light sources, such as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) or [lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29), do not emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. This also suppresses magical [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) of your _darkness_ spell's rank or lower. Light can't pass through, so creatures in the area can't see outside. From outside, it appears as a globe of pure darkness.

---
**Heightened (4th)** Even creatures with [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) (but not greater darkvision) can barely see through the darkness. They treat targets seen through the darkness as [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4).

[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 323

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

**Bloodline**
[Imperial](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=27)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies.

 You detect [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your _detect magic_ spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an [_invisibility potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=187)) typically are detected normally.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

**Heightened (4th)** As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).
name: Detect Magic

[Detonate Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

**Deities**
[Abraxas](/Deities.aspx?ID=425), [Groetus](/Deities.aspx?ID=345), [Liisglan](/Deities.aspx?ID=540), [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=288), [Wards of the Pharaoh](/Deities.aspx?ID=208)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 magic item or spell effect
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex (see text)

</row>

</column>

---

You cause the magic within the target to dissipate in a destructive explosion. You attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the target. If the attempt succeeds, an explosion of magical force deals 8d6 force damage with a basic Reflex save. If you successfully counteract the magic of an item, it's deactivated for 1 week (or destroyed on a critical success) and the explosion is a 5-foot emanation from the item. If you successfully counteract a spell, the effect ends and the explosion affects either all creatures in the spells' area or the target of the spell and all creatures in a 5-foot emanation around it.
name: Detonate Magic

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 324 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deities** [Balumbdar](/Deities.aspx?ID=674), [Sobek](/Deities.aspx?ID=642), [Valani](/Deities.aspx?ID=486), [Zevgavizeb](/Deities.aspx?ID=442) **Duration** 1 minute --- You channel the primal forces of nature to transform into a Large animal battle form, specifically that of a powerful and terrifying dinosaur. When you Cast this Spell, choose a listed battle form. You can decide the specific type of animal, but this has no effect on the form's Size or statistics. While in this form, you gain the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) and [dinosaur](/Traits.aspx?ID=45) traits. You can Dismiss the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which battle form you choose:
  • AC = 18 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 15 temporary Hit Points.
  • Low-light vision and imprecise scent 30 feet.
  • One or more [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee attacks specific to the battle form you choose, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +16, and your damage bonus is +9. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +18, unless your own is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the form you choose: - **Ankylosaurus** Speed 25 feet; **Melee** tail ([backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=173), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6 bludgeoning; **Melee** foot, **Damage** 2d6 bludgeoning. - **Brontosaurus** Speed 25 feet; **Melee** tail ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6 bludgeoning; **Melee** foot, **Damage** 2d8 bludgeoning. - **Deinonychus** Speed 40 feet; **Melee** talon ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4 piercing plus 1 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 1d10 piercing. - **Stegosaurus** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** tail ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8 piercing. - **Triceratops** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** horn, **Damage** 2d8 piercing, plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a critical hit; **Melee** foot, **Damage** 2d6 bludgeoning. - **Tyrannosaurus** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** jaws ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d12 piercing; **Melee** tail ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10 bludgeoning. --- **Heightened (5th)** Your battle form is Huge and your attacks have 15-foot reach, or 20-foot reach if they started with 15-foot reach. You instead gain 20 temporary HP, an attack modifier of +18, a damage bonus of +6, double the damage dice, and Athletics +21. **Heightened (7th)** Your battle form is Gargantuan and your attacks have 20-foot reach, or 25-foot reach if they started with 15-foot reach. You instead gain AC = 21 + your level, 25 temporary HP, an attack modifier of +25, a damage bonus of +15, double the damage dice, and Athletics +25.
name: Dinosaur Form

[Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1490)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Subtle" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=522" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 324

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[Nyarlathotep (The Veiled Voice)](/Deities.aspx?ID=575)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 target
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You shroud a creature from others' senses. The target becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), but not merely to vision. The invisibility granted by _disappearance_ applies to all precise senses an observer might have. It's still possible for a creature to find the target by [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) using various senses, looking for disturbed dust, hearing gaps in the sound spectrum, or finding some other way to discover the presence of a creature that is otherwise undetectable.
name: Disappearance

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Disguise Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 324

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[Adanye](/Deities.aspx?ID=673)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 item or spell effect
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You alter how an item's or spell's magical aura appears to effects like [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66). You can hide the auras entirely, have an item register as a common item of lower level, or make a spell register as a common spell of the same or lower rank. You can Dismiss the spell.

 A caster using _detect magic_ or [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) of a higher rank than _disguise magic_ can attempt to disbelieve the illusion using the skill matching the tradition of the spell ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) for [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) for [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) for [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) for [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)). Further attempts by the same caster get the same result as the initial check to disbelieve.

---
**Heightened (2nd)** You can Cast this Spell on a creature, disguising all items and spell effects on it.
name: Disguise Magic

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 325

**Tradition**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

**Bloodlines**
[Demonic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=21), [Imperial](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=27)

**Deities**
[Kerkamoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=140), [Ongalte](/Deities.aspx?ID=714), [Pahti Coatl](/Deities.aspx?ID=414), [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=291), [Set](/Deities.aspx?ID=641), [Smiad](/Deities.aspx?ID=522), [Sorrow's Sword](/Deities.aspx?ID=249), [The Devourer](/Deities.aspx?ID=339)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 creature, unattended object, or force construct
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Defense**
AC and [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

A black tracer bolt flies toward your target, and upon making contact intensifies into a powerful destructive beam. Make a spell attack against the target. If you hit an object or force construct (such as a [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=363)), it's destroyed with no save unless it's an [artifact](/Traits.aspx?ID=253) or similarly powerful. A single casting can destroy no more than a 10-foot cube of matter. 

If you hit a creature, it takes 12d10 damage (no damage type) with a basic Fortitude save. If you critically hit, the target gets a result one degree of success worse than the outcome of its Fortitude save. A creature reduced to 0 HP is blasted to fine powder; its gear remains.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d10.
name: Disintegrate

You unravel the magic behind a spell or effect. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the target. If you successfully counteract a magic item, it becomes a mundane item of its type for 10 minutes. This doesn't change the item's non-magical properties. If the target is an [artifact](/Traits.aspx?ID=253) or similar item, you automatically fail.
name: Dispel Magic

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 325

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 10-foot burst centered on one corner of your space

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You create an immobile globe around yourself. It attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any spell from outside the globe whose area or targets enter into it, as if the globe were a [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell 1 rank lower than its actual spell rank. If the counteract attempt succeeds, it prevents only the portion of the spell that would have entered the globe (so if the spell also has targets outside the globe, or part of its area is beyond the globe, those targets or that area are affected normally). You must form the sphere in an unbroken open space, so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost (though creatures can enter the globe after the spell is cast).
name: Dispelling Globe

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 325 **Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Bloodlines** [Angelic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=20), [Demonic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=21), [Diabolic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=22) **Area** 40-foot emanation **Defense** Fortitude **Duration** varies --- You utter a potent litany from your faith, a mandate that harms those who oppose your ideals. You deal 7d10 spirit damage to your enemies in the area; each enemy must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is enfeebled 2 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is enfeebled 2 for 1 minute. If you're on your home plane and the creature is not, the creature is sent back to its home plane. A creature of 10th level or lower must also succeed at a Will save or be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 minute; if it critically fails, it dies (this is a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect). --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d10, and the level of creatures that must attempt a second save on a critical failure increases by 2.
name: Divine Decree

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 326 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Deity** [The Godclaw](/Deities.aspx?ID=198) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Defense** Will **Duration** until your next daily preparations --- You take command of the target, forcing it to obey you. If you issue an obviously self-destructive order, the target doesn't act until you issue a new order. The effect depends on its Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is stunned 1 as it fights off your commands.
**Failure** You control the target. It gains the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) condition, but it can attempt a Will save at the end of each of its turns. On a success, the spell ends.
**Critical Failure** As a failure, but the target receives a new save only if you give it a new order that is against its nature, such as killing its allies. --- **Heightened (10th)** The duration is unlimited.
name: Dominate

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 326 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Bloodline** [Draconic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=23) **Duration** 1 minute --- Calling upon powerful magic, you gain a Large dragon battle form. When you Cast this Spell, choose one type of [common dragon or another type to which your GM allows access](DraconicBenefactors.aspx). While in this form, you gain the [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581) trait. You have hands in this battle form and can take [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. You can Dismiss the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which battle form you choose:
  • AC = 18 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 10 temporary Hit Points.
  • Speed 40 feet, fly 100 feet. You gain any of the following Speeds the chosen dragon has, but with the listed amount: burrow 20 feet, climb 40 feet, swim 60 feet.
  • Resistance 10 against the damage type of your Dragon Breath (see below).
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) and [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) [scent](/Rules.aspx?ID=417) 60 feet.
  • The following [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee attacks, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +22, and your damage bonus is +6. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead. See below for more on these attacks. **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 2d12 piercing plus 2d6 damage of the same damage type as your Dragon Breath (see below); **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d10 slashing; **Melee** tail ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10 bludgeoning.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +23, unless your own is higher.
  • **Dragon Breath** You exhale deadly magical energy in an area, dealing 10d6 damage to each creature in the area with a basic save against your spell DC. The shape, damage type, and save type match that of your chosen dragon's breath. If the chosen dragon's breath can deal more than one type of damage, choose one when you cast _dragon form_. The shape is a 30-foot cone or a 100- foot line. Once activated, Dragon Breath can't be used again for 1d4 rounds. Dragon Breath has the tradition trait matching the type of dragon and the damage trait matching the type of damage it deals, if applicable.
  • **Tradition Resistance** If the dragon's magical tradition matches that of your _dragon form_ spell, you gain the listed ability. **Arcane** resistance 5 against damage from spells; **divine** resistance 10 to spirit, vitality, and void; **occult** resistance 10 to mental; **primal** resistance 5 to physical damage.
--- **Heightened (8th)** Your battle form is Huge, you gain a +20-foot status bonus to your fly Speed, and your attacks have 10-foot reach (or 15-foot reach if they previously had 10-foot reach). You instead gain AC = 21 + your level, 15 temporary HP, an attack modifier of +28, a damage bonus of +12, and Athletics +28. Your Dragon Breath deals an additional 4d6 damage. Monster Core Dragons The dragons from [Lost Omens: Draconic Codex](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) use the following specifications for dragon form. ## Dragon Form

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 326

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** planetary

**Target** 1 creature you know by name and have met in person
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You send a message to your target's dream. The message is one-way, up to 1 minute of speech (roughly 150 words). If the  target is asleep, they receive the message instantly. If not, they receive it the next time they sleep. As soon as they receive it, the spell ends, and you know the message was sent.

---
**Heightened (4th)** You can target up to 10 creatures you know by name and have met in person. You must send the same message to all of them; the spell ends for each creature individually.
name: Dream Message

[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Aegirran](/Deities.aspx?ID=490), [Arshea](/Deities.aspx?ID=465), [Bharnarol](/Deities.aspx?ID=467), [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281), [Granduncle Taproot](/Deities.aspx?ID=665), [Saloc](/Deities.aspx?ID=547), [Shei](/Deities.aspx?ID=653), [The Anointing of Kings](/Deities.aspx?ID=722), [Tlehar](/Deities.aspx?ID=681)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 willing sleeping creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 8 hours
</row>
